Non-Defect Comment - The NFPA 71 1989 edition does not detail a test method for verifying Waterflow Alarm Service, however, section 3-4.2.1 does indicate alarm signal operation in one event of the flow of water equal to a single automatic sprinkler of the smallest orifice size installed on the system. During the audit water was flowed from the inspectors test point through an outlet which appeared to be larger than the size of sprinkler heads used on the system (with successful alarm generation). Therefore, it is the recommendation that the ASC should make verification the waterflow alarm will properly activate when the outlet is restricted down to the size of the smallest sprinkler head on the system.

On 5-20-2020, a failed to test signal was received and no action was taken by the monitoring center for ASC to investigate. NFPA 72 1999 Edition, 5-5.3.2.1.10 Each DACT shall automatically initiate and complete a test signal transmission sequence to its associated DACR at least once every 24 hours. A successful signal transmission sequence of any other type within the same 24-hour period shall fulfill the requirement to verify the integrity of the reporting system, provided signal processing is automated so that 24-hour delinquencies are individually acknowledged by supervising station personnel. NFPA 72 1999 Edition, 5-5.3.2.2.2.5 The failure to receive a test signal from the protected premises shall be treated as a trouble signal. The requirements of 5-2.6.1.4 shall apply. NFPA 72 1999 Edition, 5-2.6.1.4 (3) Provide notice, if required, to the subscriber or the authority having jurisdiction, or both, as to the nature of the interruption, the time of occurrence, and the restoration of service, when the interruption is more than 8 hours Please institute a corrective action plan to not only have the monitoring center contact you on all failed to test signals, but also to notify the AHJ when a system is wholly or partially out of service for more than 8 hours.

Administrative Comment: As noted earlier in the Records Review, this Certificate's subscriber ADIC needs to be revised to the new company name Quantum, at the same address. Compliance Issue: As discussed in detail during our inspection, this is not a 1999 Edition, NFPA 72 Central Station fire alarm system. The Listed Notifier Model AFP-200 FACP was connected to a Listed, separately installed, DCX Model STU-11Z-UL derived channel subscriber terminal unit, instead of the documented "on-board" DACT covered by the UL Listing Report. Also, the derived channel unit provided for communication to the Central Station had been field modified with a UL R/C socket mounted relay, with a contact rating of 24 Vdc, 10A for triggering the STU for communication. Field modification of any FACP with any accessories, or hardware, other than that documented in the Listing Report, voids the Listing of the product. We discussed the following options: 1.) Cancel the Certificate; 2.) Enable the 'on-board' DACT for communication on leased telephone lines as the primary communication means for Central Station service, and disconnect completely the modified derived channel unit; or, if necessary provide a Listed (unmodified) derived channel unit in place of the field modified unit, and document the unit as a 'supplementary' signaling means (on-board DACT being the primary). Please note that the City of Redmond, WA. Code Section RFDS Title: Fire Alarm Systems, Monitoring Methods dated 8/2/2005 appears to accept "dedicated leased telephone lines" under Paragraph 9.4.5.b. 3.) Verify by UL documentation (Operating and Installation Manuals) that either the AFP-200 FACP or DCX STU-11Z-UL units were determined compatible with each other for the Central Station communication application. Please let us know how you intend to proceed.

Compliance Issue: As discussed earlier in this report, this is not a 1999 Edition, NFPA 72 Central Station fire alarm system. The Listed Edwards System Technologies (EST) Model 3 FACP was connected to a Listed, separately installed, Versus Technology Model STU-11Z-UL derived channel subscriber terminal unit, instead of the documented "on-board" DACT covered by the UL Listing Report. We discussed the following options: 1.) Cancel the Certificate; 2.) Enable the 'on-board' Model EST-3 DACT for communication on leased telephone lines as the primary communication means for Central Station service, and disconnect completely the derived channel unit; or, if necessary keep the Listed (unmodified) derived channel unit in place, but document the unit as a 'supplementary' signaling means (on-board DACT being the primary). 3.) Verify by UL documentation (Operating and Installation Manuals) that either the EST-3 FACP or Versus Technology Model STU-11Z-UL units were determined compatible with each other for the Central Station communication application. Please let us know how you intend to proceed.

Protection of Fire Alarm Control Unit(s). In areas that are not continuously occupied, automatic smoke detection shall be provided at the location of each fire alarm control unit(s) to provide notification of fire at that location. Exception: Where ambient conditions prohibit installation of automatic smoke detection, automatic heat detection shall be permitted. NFPA 72, 1999 Edition 1-5.6 The intent of 1-5.6 is to have the fire alarm system respond before it is incapacitated by fire. There have been several fatal fires where the origin and path of the fire resulted in destruction of the control unit before a detector responded. A-1-5.6 It has long been approved for the Smoke (Heat) Detector to be placed directly above the panel within two feet in the Memphis Jurisdiction. The heat is currently placed approximately 4 feet below the ceiling (about 6-8 feet above the panel) where smoke and heat can move directly past it without triggering the detector. Detectors are intended to be placed at the highest point so that they can have smoke and heat build up around the unit and enter the chamber to trigger an alarm. The above referenced information should allow you to lower the detector to directly above the panel so that the intention of the device is met.

Non-defect comment: NFPA 72 (1999 edition) does not require that all audible notification appliances within a building be of the same type (ref A.4-3.1.1). A mixture of different types within a space is not the desired method. With the use of both horn and temporal code signals in the building, such things as occupant training, signage and drills can be done to avoid confusion.

Per NFPA 72 section 1-5.4.7 (1999 edition), Audible alarm notification appliances for fire alarm shall produce signals that are distinctive from other similar appliances used for other purposes in the same area. Fire alarm sound shall not be used for any other purpose. The burglar alarm siren sounds with the fire alarm horns. If the burglar siren is used with the fire alarm, then it can not be used on burglar alarm. The use of the burglar alarm siren should be discontinued to be used with the fire alarm horns.

NFPA 72, 1999 Edition, section 5-2.2.2 Section 5-2 shall apply to central station service, which consists of the following elements: (1) Installation of fire alarm transmitters (2) Alarm, guard, supervisory, and trouble signal monitoring (3) Retransmission (4) Associated record keeping and reporting (5) Testing and maintenance (6) Runner service The central station service elements shall be provided under contract to a subscriber by one of the following: (c) A listed fire alarm service–local company that provides the installation and testing and maintenance with its own facilities and personnel and that subcontracts the monitoring, retransmission, and associated record keeping and reporting to a listed central station. The required runner service shall be provided by the listed fire alarm service–local company with its own personnel or the listed central station with its own personnel. **During the audit it was found that the executed contracts do not reflect that runner service will be provided. Please either revise the current contract or add an addendum listing runner service as part of the provided central station services.

On 2/8/13 at 1:26, there was a trouble signal sent. The note of runner service arriving was at 7:25. Per NFPA 72 section 5-2.6.1.4 (1999 edition) a runner must arrive within 4 hours for a trouble signal.

On 4/15/11 (Friday) at 10:32 a Late to Test trouble signal was received. No record of a runner arriving within 4 hours is seen in the signal record as required per NFPA 72 5-2.6.1.4 (1999 edition). Record indicated customer did not want service until Monday, which the record appears to indicated service person was on site at that time. Please take steps to ensure your customer does not cause non-compliance with meeting code requirements for runner response. Also, the Late to Test signal restore was recorded at 19:31, (8 hours and 59 minutes) it is required that the central station provide notice to the AHJ as to the nature of the interruption, the time of occurrence, and restoration of service, when the interruption is more than 8 hours. Nothing was seen in the record to indicate that the AHJ was notified.

On 5/22/21 at 17:39, there was a trouble signal sent. The note of runner service arriving was not at the time required due to the customer not opening the business to meet the runner. Per NFPA 72 section 5-2.6.1.4 (1999 edition) a runner must arrive within 4 hours for a trouble signal and then notify the AHJ of the nature of the issue if not restored within 8 hours. Please advise your customer of these requirements.

The Minnesota State Fire Code adopted the NFPA 2002 edition requiring a two hour runner response time for Alarm and Supervisory signals. Whenever you re-issue a certificate for a protected premise that the system is certified as installed and operated in accordance with standard NFPA 72, 1999 edition OR earlier, please add to the section on system deviations from the referenced standard, that "The AHJ allows for two hour runner response time for alarm and supervisory signals."

Where more than two visible notification appliances are in the same field of view, they shall flash in synchronization. (Reference: NFPA 72, 2002 Edition, Section 7.5.4.1.2 (3)). With Primary power turned off to the alarm system, it was noted that at least 5 horn/strobe notification appliances were not operating and the remaining devices did not flash in synchronization. Per section 4.4.1.5.4, operation on secondary power shall not affect the required performance of a fire alarm system. The system shall produce the same signals and indications when operating from the secondary power source.

Total Coverage as declared on the Certificate shall include all rooms, halls, storage areas, basements, attics, lofts, spaces above suspended ceilings, and other subdivisions and accessible spaces as well as the inside of all closets, elevator shafts, enclosed stairways, dumbwaiter shafts, and chutes. Either add additional detectors to meet the requirments of "TOTAL" Coverage, or cancel Certificate and reissue the Certificate as "Partial' Coverage. Please refer to NFPA 72, 2002 Edition and the local Authority Having Jurisdiction.

Total Smoke Detection Coverage as declared on the Certificate shall include all rooms, halls, storage areas, basements, attics, lofts, spaces above suspended ceilings, and other subdivisions and accessible spaces as well as the inside of all closets, elevator shafts, enclosed stairways, dumbwaiter shafts, and chutes. Please re-issue Certificate to reflect the actual type of coverage provided. Per NFPA 72 2002 paragraph 5.5.2.1.

Upon audit review, no UL Certificate was found within 3' of the FACU as required. Please update the certificate, reissue and post at the protected premise (NFPA 72-2002: 8.2.4 / 8.2.4.1.2 & City of San Francisco Fire Department requirements).

Upon audit review, no indication was provided by the Alarm Service Company (ASC) representative confirming proper access to and utilization of NFPA 72 particularly the 2002 Edition to which the installed system compliance is declared through the issued UL Certificate – Please confirm and provide access verification to NFPA 72 (electronic or possession of edition(s)) and appropriate code resources for qualifications regarding fire alarm system.

Upon audit review, the fire sprinkler system was not supervised for supervisory signals. Two separate and distinct signals shall be initiated: one indicating movement of the valve from its normal position (off-normal), and the other indicating restoration of the valve to its normal position (NFPA 72-2002: 5.13.1.1) Please provide or submit AHJ approval indicating deviation from NFPA 72-2002 is acceptable. Additionally, if AHJ approval is provided, the existing lack of sprinkler supervisory devices shall be noted as a deviation on the issued Certificate.

When activated, the inspectors test valve shall have a flow of water equal to the smallest orifice size head installed on the system. The inspectors test outlet appears not to exist on this system. Please provide AHJ accepted deviation of alternate test method, otherwise the certificate must be cancelled without indication of a proper inspectors test point in use. Refer to NFPA 72 table 10.4.2.2 paragraph 13 (I) (2002 edition)

When listing alarm noification devices on certificates and the installation of horn/strobes shall comply with the code NFPA 72 2002 chapter 7or shall listed as a deviation on the certificate with documentation from local AHJ.

During review of the signal processing history, the system was found to have reoccurring alarm signals from multiple instances and devices throughout the 90 day history. As per NFPA 72, 2002, section 10.2, please assess the cause of the reoccurring alarm signals and confirm device and system stability with your clearance letter.

Please Reference NFPA 72 2002 Edition, Sec 4.5.1.2 Before requesting final approval of the installation, if required by the authority having jurisdiction, the installing contractor shall furnish a written statement stating that the system has been installed in accordance with approved plans and tested in accordance with the manufacturer’s specifications and the appropriate NFPA requirements ** During the documentation review it was noted there was no Record of Completion (ROC) provided. Separation of non power-limited and power-limited circuits must be maintained within the control enclosure. Refer to NFPA 72, 2002 Edition, Paragraph 4.4.4.4 and NEC Article 760.

The initiation of an alarm did not occur within 90 seconds of actuating waterflow at the time of audit, as per NFPA 72, 2002, paragraph 5.10.2. Please determine the cause of this and make the necessary changes to bring this element of the system into compliance, and confirm with your clearance letter.

A supervisory condition, shall be annunciated at the protected property, visually and audibly, per NFPA72 2002 edition section 4.4.3.2.3. The sprinkler valve supervision signal activated the alarm strobes at the premise. Supervisory signals at the premise may only be indicated at the FACP or other building control communication center and not activate occupant evacuation notification appliances.

About half way through the audit, the FACP's DACT began indicating communication failure, and after some trouble shooting it was not clear as to why this was occurring. Please investigate and begin all necessary repairs and verify in your replay that the DACT is installed and functioning incompliance per NFPA72 2002 edition section 8.5.3.2.1.

Any general trouble or supervisory condition, shall be annunciated at the protected property, visually and audibly, per NFPA72 2002 edition sections 4.4.3.5.6(1) and 4.4.3.2.3(1). The simulated phone line, NAC trouble, and sprinkler valve supervision were not audibly indicated at the premise.

Cellular only transmitters, as provided for this certificate, are not permitted per NFPA 72 2002 edition. However, NFPA 72 2013 edition, per section 26.6.3.1.4.1 allows; unless prohibited by the AHJ, a single transmission path shall be permitted and the path shall be supervised at an interval of not more than 60 minutes (similarly the NFPA 72 2010 edition permits with a 5 minute supervision interval). A failure of the path shall be annunciated at the supervising station and at the protected premise. Please verify which frequency of transmission path supervision is being used and reissue the certificate to the appropriate edition of NFPA 72.
Duct detectors that are transmitting supervisary signals to monitoring center shall be tested quarterly in accordance with NFPA 72 2002 pg99 par 15 I.

During review of the signal processing history, the system was found to have runaway signals from multiple instances and devices throughout the 90 day history. Runaway signals compromise the integrity of the FACU's ability to send accurate signals, as well as have the ability to compromise the integrity of the monitoring center's processing of signals. As per NFPA 72, 2002, paragraph 10.2.2.4, please assess the cause of the runaway signals and ring the signals into compliance with UL 1981.

During the audit it was noted that all signals transmitted to the proprietary monitoring station are through an external transmitter interface noted as an Dantel Alarm System. No information was found in my research to indicate this was a UL listed system for either Fire Alarms or Information Technology Equipment. I have also noted that the ATT&T Greenville TX station UL Listing (S24557) approved only Bosch D6600 receivers for UL Certificated accounts in conjunction with Bold automation system. Otherwise receiving and processing signals from an UL Certificated accounts also does not appear to be in compliance with NFPA 72 (2002 edition) section 8.3.4.2.2. This certificate needs to be cancelled until all signals can be transmitted and processed b UL Approved Equipment.

During the audit it was noted that the Honeywell IPGSM-4G external transmitter was in use and only the digital cellular communicator capability was being utilized. Upon investigation it was determined that that this was an acceptable configuration within the Listing of the device. However, the supervision requirements of the NFPA 72 edition in effect, and the manufacture installation requirements must also be met. The certificate is currently issued to the 2002 edition of NFPA 72, which, per section 8.5.4.4 requires any failure of communication with the supervising station shall be indicated within 5 minutes. A field test resulted in failure being indicated at the monitoring station after 50 minutes. The unit must either be programmed to indicate communication failure after five minutes, or the AHJ give you a written approved deviation, or the certificate issued as the system is compliant to the 2013 edition of NFPA 72. Also. Per the manufacture's installation instructions, the phone lines from the FACP DACT to the external transmitter must be in conduit.

During the audit signal review, it took 3 minutes or more for some signals to be received at the monitoring station. Per NFPA 72, (2002 edition) section 8.5.3.2.1.3(B), the DACT should be able to transmit to the DACR within 90 seconds. It had appeared the DACT was causing phone line issues or failing to operate properly. Please verify proper operation.

During the audit signal review, it took 3 minutes or more for some signals to be received at the monitoring station. Each telephone line on the DACT shall dial a separate phone number for the DACR per NFPA 72, 2002 edition, paragraph 8.5.3.2.1.5 (5).

At a minimum, the verification shall ensure that the installed system includes all required components and functions, that those components and functions are installed and operate as required, that the system has been 100-percent acceptance tested in accordance with Chapter 10, and that all required documentation has been provided to the system owner. For supervising station systems, the verification shall also ascertain proper arrangement, transmission, and receipt of all signals required to be transmitted off-premises. Exception: Where the installation is an extension, modification, or reconfiguration of an existing system, the verification shall be required for the new work only and reacceptance testing in accordance with Chapter 10 shall be acceptable. Non-defect comment: NFPA 72 (2002 edition) does not require that all audible notification appliances within a building be of the same type (ref A.7.4.1.1). A mixture of different types within a space is not the desired method. With the use of both horn and temporal code signals in the building, such things as occupant training, signage and drills can be done to avoid confusion.

Power for the FACU was not hardwired to a power source on a dedicated circuit. NFPA 72, 2002 Edition, Sections 4.4.1.4.1 Dedicated Branch Circuit. A dedicated branch circuit of one of the following shall supply primary power: (1) Commercial light and power. (3) An engine-driven generator or equivalent arranged for cogeneration with commercial light and power in accordance with 4.4.1.9.2, where a person specifically trained in its operation is on duty at all times. 4.4.1.4.2 Mechanical Protection. 4.4.1.4.2.1 The dedicated branch circuit(s) and connections shall be mechanically protected. 4.4.1.4.2.2 Circuit disconnecting means shall have a red marking, shall be accessible only to authorized personnel, and shall be identified as “FIRE ALARM CIRCUIT.”

A surprise service test was conducted from the protected premise at Haven Crest Alf 2800 NW 25th Way, Boca Raton, FL. This test was conducted in the presence of a representative from the Boca Raton, FL Fire Department. The test was initiated at 9:27 am by testing a manual pull station at the premise. The Boca Raton Fire Department was dispatched by the subcontracted Central Station at 9:28 am of the Fire Alarm Signal. A runner/service technician arrived at the protected premise test site at 10:32 am. As a result, this test is considered in compliance with NFPA 72 2002 Edition.

Between 1/21/23 and 3/26/23 five Low air pressure supervisory signal events were received. The restoral was not received for more than two hours in all cases.. Per NFPA 72 (2002 edition) section 8.2.7.3, the operator is required to communicate immediately with the subscriber to determine if the dispatch of a service runner is necessary. Runner service within 2 hours at the premise for unrestored supervisory signals is required and after 8 hours notification to the AHJ. Steps need to be taken to ensure compliance with these requirements.

Currently there is a maintenance agreement between the subscriber and the maintenance provider for the alarm system. A maintenance, inspection, testing, and runner service agreement is required between Alarm Monitoring Center and the subcontractor of these services. Refer to NFPA 72 paragraph 8.2.3 (2002 edition)

During record review there was not a contract in the folder. NFPA 72 2002 edition,8.2.3 Contract Requirements. The central station service elements shall be provided under contract to a subscriber by one of the following: (4) A listed central station that provides the installation, testing, and maintenance with its own facilities and personnel and that subcontracts the monitoring, retransmission, and associated record keeping and reporting to another listed central station. The required runner service shall be provided by either central station.

In the 4 month signal history provided, 5 daily test signals "with trouble" were received. No record of runner dispatch or arrival at the site within 4 hours as required per NFPA 72, 2002 Edition, section 8.2.7.4.

It is required to provide for all aspects of Central Station Service under contract or agreement. This contract shall be in place between the UL Certificate issuing alarm service company (ASC) and the subscriber at the protected property. If any aspect of the service, maintenance or runner response is provided by a third party, a sub-contract for those services shall be in place between the ASC and that third party (NFPA 72, 2002 Edition, Paragraphs 8.2.2., A.8.2.2, and 8.2.3). These contracts shall be made available for review.

It is required to provide for all elements of Central Station Service under contract or agreement. On 1/15/12 at 9:59 a trouble signal was received. The restoral was not received until 1/17/12 at 13:41. No operator action was noted in the signal history provided for this event.

On 12/26/13 at 10:21 a Backflow, West end of Building, supervisory signal was received. The restoral was not received until 1/2/13. Per NFPA 72 (2002 edition) section 8.2.7.3, the operator is required to communicate immediately with the subscriber to determine if the dispatch of a service runner is necessary. No operator action was noted in the signal history provided for this event. Runner service within 2 hours at the premise for unrestored supervisory signals is required and after 8 hours notification to the AHJ. Steps need to be taken to ensure compliance with these requirements.

The Certificate shall be subject to cancellation unless the following code requirement or options can be met. NFPA 72 (2002) 8.2.3 (3) Contract Requirements - A listed fire alarm service local company that provides the installation, testing, and maintenance with its own facilities and personnel and that subcontracts the monitoring, retransmission, and associated record keeping and reporting to a listed central station. The required runner service shall be provided by the listed fire alarm service local company with its own personnel or the listed central station with its own personnel. Alternative option one; A deviation may be accepted with prior written approval of the local AHJ. This shall be declared on the Certificate indicating code deficiency with the written approval of the AHJ kept on file in your records. Alternative option two; The Certificate may be reissued under the UUJS category as a Local or Remote Station service with a local alarm service company that also holds a UUJS Listing with the written approval of the local AHJ.

The last four months of signals were reviewed. Five alarm and 24 trouble signals were received, two fire and five trouble signals were not handled appropriately. No Supervisory signals were received. A) On 1/23/10, 08:32 a Fire signal was received. The operator made calls to verify the alarm before dispatching the fire department and should have followed NFPA 72 (2002) section 8.2.7.1.2.(1) B) On 2/23/10, 02:41 a Fire signal was received with the restoral received at 07:28. A runner did not arrive to restore the signal within two hours as required by NFPA 72 (2002) section 8.2.7.1.2.(2) C) On 2/4/10, 07:33 a comm. loss line 1 trouble signal was received with the restoral received at 07:39. No operator communication with the subscriber occurred as required per NFPA 72 (2002) section 8.2.7.4.(1). D) On 2/4/10, 10:30 a zone 137 trouble signal was received with the restoral received at 10:38. No operator communication with the subscriber occurred as required per NFPA 72 (2002) section 8.2.7.4.(1). E) On 2/10/10, 03:45 a zone 137 trouble signal was received with the restoral received at 07:17. No operator communication with the subscriber occurred as required per NFPA 72 (2002) section 8.2.7.4.(1). F) On 3/4/10, 18:47 a zone 137 trouble signal was received with the restoral received at 07:16 on 3/5/10. The operator communicated with the subscriber, but no runner was dispatched or arrived within 4 hours as required per NFPA 72 (2002) section 8.2.7.4.(2). G) On 4/5/10, 09:41 a comm. loss line 1 trouble signal was received with the restoral received at 10:09. Operator communication with the subscriber occurred at 09:57, not as required per NFPA 72 (2002) section 8.2.7.4.(1). Note: Per the Annex A of NFPA 72, "immediately" means without reasonable delay, but within the context of its use is a maximum of 90 seconds for alarm signals and a maximum of four minutes for supervisory and trouble signals.

There shall be a contract in place between the alarm service company and the building owner providing runner service to the certificated installation. Please refer to NFPA 72 2002 Edition, Paragraph 8.3.2 Item #3.

Upon arrival it was determined that the Fire Alarm Control Panel was not communicating with the monitoring station. In reviewing the signal history for this system, it is noted that the panel suffered loss of AC power on September 9th then on September 11th sent multiple low battery signals until all communication stopped. There were no additional signals recorded until the day of this audit visit on November 14th. No troubles were indicated on the panel upon arrival. Trouble shooting revealed that all programing in the panel was loss, so no monitoring station phone numbers were in the panel. Local alarm annunciation at the panel and to the alarm signaling devices were working properly. Upon departure, the panel communication with the monitoring station was restored. Signal history records indicated that automated emails were sent to both the Alarm Dealer and to the system subscriber. The automated system also noted that the dealer email was unsuccessful but the subscriber's email was successful. In addition, the signal history does not record that the Central Station's automation system reported that there were any missing 24-hour test signals for this account during the entire time of complete power loss up to restored communicaions during the audit. To resolve these items in your clearance letter, please work with your monitoring station in providing an action plan to address all of the following: 1) Ensuring the station's ability to dispatch runner or service persons to arrive on premise within the response time required, including a monitoring station continued response when it is determined the runner dispatch has failed as it appears that all attempts to respond to these signals in this case were automated and no operator action ever became recorded in the signal history (Ref NFPA 72 section 8.2.7.4, 2002 edition), and if the impairment extends beyond 8 hours then the authority having jurisdiction is notified of the issue and the restoral once provided. 2) Ensuring the station's ability for each DACT to successfully record a signal transmission every 24 hours when provided that the signal processing is automated so that 24-hour delinquencies are individually acknowledged and handled by the station operators (Ref NFPA 72 section 8.5.3.2.1.5 (7), 2002 edition).

The maximum duration between the initiation of an alarm signal at the protected premises, transmission of the signal, and subsequent display and recording of the alarm signal at the supervising station shall not exceed 90 seconds. (NFPA 72, 2002 Edition, Par. 8.5.4.7).

Actuation of the alarm notification appliances shall occur within 10 seconds after the activation of an initiating device (NFPA 72, 2002 Edition, Par. 6.8.1.1 and A.6.8.11). There is a 2-minute delay from the time the smoke detector activates (causes an alarm on the FACU, and reports the alarm) until the notification appliances energize.

A Trouble on the NAC panel will not cause a trouble condition at the FACP. All circuits must be Monitored for integrity. Please correct this situation. Ref NFPA 72, 2002 ed, section 4.4.7.1.

According to the TG7 maunufacturer instructions the TG7 panel must be monitored for troubles at the FACP. A low battery condition was not reported to the FACP when tested. Please install or wire the TG7 panel to send trouble signals to the FACP so that local annunication of a trouble will be reported. 1-5.1.2 Equipment. Equipment constructed and installed in conformity with this code shall be listed for the purpose for which it is used. 1-5.4.6 Trouble Signals. Trouble signals and their restoration to normal shall be indicated within 200 seconds at the locations identified in 1-5.4.6.1 or 1-5.4.6.2. Trouble signals required to indicate at the protected premises shall be indicated by distinctive audible signals. These audible trouble signals shall be distinctive from alarm signals. If an intermittent signal is used, it shall sound at least once every 10 seconds, with a minimum duration of 1/2 second. An audible trouble signal shall be permitted to be common to several supervised circuits. The trouble signal(s) shall be located in an area where it is likely to be heard. 1-5.4.6.1 Visible and audible trouble signals and visible indication of their restoration to normal shall be indicated at the following locations: (1) Control unit (central equipment) for protected premises fire alarm systems (2) Building fire command center for emergency voice/alarm communications service (3) Central station or remote station location for systems installed in compliance with Chapter 5 NFPA 72 2002 Edition

All trouble signal need to be processed by operators in central station . Refer to NFPA72 2002. 8.2.7.4 Trouble Signals. Upon receipt of trouble signals or other signals pertaining solely to matters of equipment maintenance of the fire alarm systems, the central station shall perform the following actions: (1) *Communicate immediately with persons designated by the subscriber (2) Dispatch personnel to arrive within 4 hours to initiate maintenance, if necessary ( 3 ) Provide notice, if required, to the subscriber or the authority having jurisdiction, or both, as to the nature of the interruption, the time of occurrence, and the restoration of service, when the interruption is more than 8 hours.

All trouble signals shall have a means arranged as to be transmitted to the supervising station, reference NFPA 72 (2002 edition) Sections 6.12.3 and 8.4.2.2. During the audit, the trouble signal generated for the manual pull station zone was not received at your monitoring station.

All trouble signals shall have a means arranged as to be transmitted to the supervising station, reference NFPA 72 (2002 edition) Sections 6.12.3 and 8.4.2.2. During the audit, trouble signals were not received at your receiving station.

Any general trouble condition, shall be annunciated at the protected property, visually and audibly, per NFPA72 2002 edition sections 4.4.3.5.6(1). The simulated phone line trouble and NAC trouble were not audibly indicated at the premise. The Record of Completion, signed by the AHJ, and the deviations to the Standard section of the issued certificate, indicates that the system has regular dialer troubles due to multiple panels on the same phone line. However, it is not clear that the AHJ approved disabling the audible panel signals for these conditions.

During our visit, a ground fault condition was generated without the Central Station being notified beforehand. The FACU immediately went into trouble and indicated the ground fault condition and dialed out the trouble signal to the Central Station as required. After waiting over 16 minutes with no response from the Central Station, a call was made verifying signal activity for this account. The operator verified the receipt of the signal and that no actions were taken to retransmit the signal as required. In fact the operators tone indicated that no further actions were required. In your response to this report, please verify that you have corresponded with the appropiate parties at your Central Station and that they clearly understand the code requirement for this and that they will take the appropriate steps as neccesary to minimize the likelyhood of this problen re-occuring. Please refer to NFPA 72 2002 paragraph 8.2.7.4 and the annex material fot this subject.

During the audit process, Telco line #2 was used to generate signals from the protected premise. However, no signals transmitted to the central station. Additionally, no trouble signal was observed/heard at panel regarding Telco line #2 failure. Upon ASC technician examining line #2, continuity and functionality of the phone line were confirmed. After further analysis, Telco line #2 was never programmed as a secondary means of signal transmission by the ASC. Required testing of communication methods is required by NFPA 72. ITM records provided do not indicate required testing completion. Failure of either of the DACT's telephone lines shall be annunciated at the protected property and a trouble signal transmitted to the supervising station within 4 minutes of the fault's detection, per (NFPA 72-2002: 8.5.3.2.1.4 / 8.5.3.2.1.5 / 10.4.2.2.16 (a) (b)). * Corrected at audit. ASC representative in office reprogrammed Telco #2 into alarm sequence of operations.

The waterflow and a tamper are wired into the same zone. The way the panel is wired currently causes the tamper to send a trouble signal. Also, a tamper is not a trouble condidtion. See 3.3.171.6 Supervisory Signal. A signal indicating the need for action in connection with the supervision of guard tours, the fire suppression systems or equipment, or the maintenance features of related systems. 4.4.3.6.2 The distinction among signals shall be as follows: (1) Fire alarm signals shall be distinctive in sound from other signals, shall comply with the requirements of 6.8.6.4.1, and their sound shall not be used for any other purpose. (2)*Supervisory signals shall be distinctive in sound from other signals, and their sound shall not be used for any other purpose. Exception: A supervisory signal sound shall be permitted to be used to indicate a trouble condition. If the same sound is used for both supervisory signals and trouble signals, the distinction between signals shall be by other appropriate means such as visible annunciation. (3) Fire alarm, supervisory, and trouble signals shall take precedence, in that respective order of priority, over all other signals. 8.3.5.6.3 Supervisory Signals. Upon receipt of sprinkler system and other supervisory signals, the proprietary supervising station operator shall initiate action to perform the following, if required: (1) Communicate immediately with the designated person(s) to ascertain the reason for the signal (2) Dispatch personnel to arrive within 2 hours to investigate, unless supervisory conditions are promptly restored (3) Notify the fire department (4) Notify the authority having jurisdiction when sprinkler systems are wholly or partially out of service for 8 hours or more (5) Provide written notice to the authority having jurisdiction as to the nature of the signal, time of occurrence, and restoration of service, when equipment has been out of service for 8 hours or more. NFPA 72 2002 edition.

The following Certificates were selected for 2011 audit, and exibited almost identical deficiencies as noted below: 1.) Certificate # FC99555602 for Plaza East Assoc. 1300 Buchanan St. SFO, CA. 2.) Certificate # FC97719381 for Plaza East Assoc. 1250 Buchanan St. SFO, CA. and 3.) Certificate # FC99872331 for Plaza East Assoc. 1248 Buchanan St. SFO, CA. All had written active contracts. However, none of the three had a record of the annual inspection and test required by the 2007 Edition of NFPA 72, Chapter 10, Table 10.4.2.2. In addition, all three did not have any As-Built drawings in the office file as required by the 2007 Edition of NFPA72, Paragraph 10.6.1.1. Similarly, a review of the automated MASMastermind database program alarm records for the January 1 through February 28, 2011 time preriod revealed an unusual absence of typically received signal activity, including the required 24 hour test report timer transmission required by Paragraph 8.6.3.21.5. Lack of signaling led us to suspect that the three systems were either 'out-of-service' for some reason, or the subscriber(or ADT, the service provider) had terminated the Central Station alarm monitoring service. Following, we visited the protected premises on buchannan St. and discusssed the issue with the Plaza East Assoc. LP property manager. The property manager for all three locations noted above advised that the properties were no longer protected by ADT - So. SFO, but by Cintas Corp. San Jose, CA. Based on the findings above, we request that ADT immediately cancel Certificate numbers: FC99555602, FC97719381, and FC99872331.

A notification appliance is required for systems intended to operate in the public mode. Fire alarm and mass notification systems provided for evacuation or relocation of occupants shall have one or more notification appliances listed for the purpose on each floor of the building and so located such that they have the characteristics described in Chapter 7 for public mode or private mode, as required.

In spaces served by air-handling systems, detectors shall not be located where airflow prevents the operation of the detector (NFPA 72, 2007 Edition, Paragraphs 5.7.4.1 and A.5.7.4.1). There are smoke detectors located less than 3' from air vents. Installed directly on return air vents were open area Spot Type Smoke Detector. Per NFPA 72 2007 ed. Sections 5.16.5.1, 5.16.5.6, and 5.7.3.2.3.2: Detectors shall be Listed for the purpose they are being used and for operation over the complete range of air velocities expected, and all manufacturer's installation instructions must be followed. Without manufacturer's documentation supporting the application as installed, you will need an AHJ written approval for this and indicate such on the certificate.

When activated, the inspectors test valve shall have a flow of water equal to the smallest orifice size head installed on the system. The inspectors test outlet appears not to exist on this system. Please provide AHJ accepted deviation of alternate test method, otherwise the certificate must be cancelled without indication of a proper inspectors test point in use. Refer to NFPA 72, 2007 Edition, Table 10.4.2.2 - 14i.

During review of the signal processing history, the system was found to have reoccurring alarm signals from multiple instances and devices throughout the 90 day history. As per NFPA 72, 2007, section 10.2, please assess the cause of the reoccurring alarm signals and confirm devices and system are free of defects or malfunctions with your clearance letter.

Cellular only transmitters, as provided for this certificate, are not permitted per NFPA 72 2007 edition. However, NFPA 72 2013 edition, per section 26.6.3.1.4.1 allows; unless prohibited by the AHJ, a single transmission path shall be permitted and the path shall be supervised at an interval of not more than 60 minutes (similarly the NFPA 72 2010 edition permits with a 5 minute supervision interval). A failure of the path shall be annunciated at the supervising station and at the protected premise. Please verify which frequency of transmission path supervision is being used and reissue the certificate to the appropriate edition of NFPA 72 or add a second transmission path.

During the audit signal review, the several signals generated during the testing, took more than 3 minutes to be received at the monitoring station. Per NFPA 72, (2007 edition) section 8.6.3.2.1.3, the DACT shall be able to seize the phone line and transmit to the DACR within 90 seconds. Please recheck to verify proper operation.

During the audit signal review, the waterflow alarm generated at 9:51 during the testing, failed to be received at the monitoring station. Per NFPA 72, (2007 edition) section 8.6.3.2.1.3(B), the DACT should be able to transmit to the DACR within 90 seconds. Please recheck to verify proper operation.

During the evaluation, a manual pull box fire alarm signal was generated while the primary phone line was disabled. This signal was not received at the central monitoring station. (Ref NFPA 72 2007, section 8.3.7.5.1 and section 8.6.3.2.1.5(2) for a DACT being able to select the operable line in the event of failure of the other).

During the evaluation, manual pull box fire alarm signals were generated while the primary phone line was disabled. These signals were not received at the central monitoring station of record. The supplementary cellular digital transmitter at the site only transmits signals to the Regions Bank (property owner) monitoring center, which is where the manual alarm signals were sent. The cellular communicator was removed from the configuration and retested. The signals were then properly sent to Diebold's central station via the second phone line. (Ref NFPA 72 2007, section 8.3.7.5.3)

During the evaluation, signals were generated while the system had been placed in test mode. When calling back to check on signal receipt, it was reported that the Walgreen's monitoring center was down and not receiving any signals on their network. Per NFPA 72 2007, section 8.3.7.5.3 any test signals not received are investigated and actions taken to reestablish communication. Please reply with a brief description of the station's problems to receive signals and the resolution.
Failure of telephone lines (numbers) or cellular service shall be annunciated locally. 8.6.3.2.1.4 (B) NFPA 72 2007 edition. The AES is not set up for troubles to annunciate locally through the FACP.

The Follow-up Service Agreement that your company executed indicates that the Alarm Service Company assumes full and complete responsibility for its use of the UL Mark, in this case UL Certificates. Managing your customers UL Certificates is necessary to insure that a UL Certificate covers only active systems that comply with AHJ and NFPA 72 requirements and are maintained by your Service Center. In the event a Certificated fire alarm system is not able to be brought into compliance with the AHJ and NFPA 72 nation fire alarm and signaling code, The Certificate should be cancelled within 30 days and a new Certificate may be issued when system interruptions are repaired and the fire alarm system has been restored to a normal condition. Maintenance of the fire alarm system is the responsibility of the property owner or building owner or the owner's designated representative. Ref. NFPA 72 (2007) 10.2.2 Responsibilities. 10.2.2.1* The property or building owner or the owner’s designated representative shall be responsible for inspection, testing, and maintenance of the system and for alterations or additions to this system. 10.2.2.2 The delegation of responsibility shall be in writing, with a copy of such delegation provided to the authority having jurisdiction upon request. 10.2.2.3 Inspection, testing, or maintenance shall be permitted to be done by a person or organization other than the owner if conducted under a written contract. 10.2.2.4 Testing and maintenance of central station service systems shall be performed under the contractual arrangements specified in 8.3.3.

The restaurant utilizes a fire suppression system for cooking operations. Where required to be monitored and a building fire alarm system is installed, the actuation of a fire suppression system shall annunciate an alarm or supervisory condition at the building fire alarm control unit. Please verify appropriate connection, functionality and inspection, testing and maintenance (ITM) and provide documentation (NFPA 72-2007: 6.8.5.7.1).

The sprinkler system control valve is being supervised with a device Listed for use only on special applications, such as pit control valves where the control stem does not rise/fall with use. Per NFPA 72 (2007 edition) section 5.15.1.3 the off-normal signal shall not be restored at any valve position except normal. This type of supervisory device can be re-set indicating a normal condition at the fire panel but the control valves may be off-normal. Use of this type of device must be approved as an accepted deviation from the standard by the AHJ and reflected on the certificate or the valves shall be supervised by a properly Listed device for the application. Please issue a replacement certificate indicating the sprinkler supervisory valve and the AHJ approved deviation of accepting the use of a device not listed for its application.

Non-defect comment: NFPA 72 (2007 edition) does not require that all audible notification appliances within a building be of the same type (ref A.7.4.1.1). A Fire Service Test was conducted at this property on Thursday April 18, 2019 at 13:46. The result of the service test is as follows: The runner failed to arrive within the required response time. Please provide in writing the cause and the resolution. Refer to NFPA 72, 2007 Ed. 8.3.7.1 Because of the failure of the runner to arrive within the required response time a project will be opened to conduct a Supplemental Fire Service Test within the next 60 days. The cost of the supplemental service test will be $1980.00 plus travel & living expenses. If the Supplemental Fire Service Test also yields noncompliant results, UL will have no choice but to suspend your ability to issue or renew certificates until such time as effective process changes can demonstrate that compliance has been achieved.

During signal processing review, It appears that the protected premise property management firm performs Runners Service upon activation of fire alarm signals. Runner service requirements were explained to the Alarm Service Company (ASC) during review of signal processing records. The ASC was advised of their requirements for resetting/maintaining equipment, qualifications to perform work or service on a fire alarm system as well as the prescribed time frames for arrival at the protect premise regarding central station service signal dispositions. (NFPA 72-2007: 8.3.2 / 8.3.3 / 8.3.7.1 / 8.3.7.3 / 8.3.7.5).

For 9/2/17 to 11/10/17 Signal history records indicated that automated emails were sent to both the Alarm Dealer and to the system subscriber due to meny off normal, trouble and supervisory signal during this time. The automated system also noted that the dealer email was unsuccessful but the subscriber's email was successful. In addition, the signal history does not record that the Central Station's automation system reported that there were any missing 24-hour test signals for this account during the time when no communicaions signals were sent. To resolve these items in your clearance letter, please work with your monitoring station in providing an action plan to address all of the following: 1) Ensuring the station's ability to dispatch runner or service persons to arrive on premise within the response time required, including a monitoring station continued response when it is determined the runner dispatch has failed as it appears that all attempts to respond to these signals in this case were automated and no operator action ever became recorded in the signal history (Ref NFPA 72 section 8.3.7.4, 2007 edition), and if the impairment extends beyond 8 hours then the authority having jurisdiction is notified of the issue and the restoral once provided. 2) Ensuring the station's ability for each DACT to successfully record a signal transmission every 24 hours when provided that the signal processing is automated so that 24-hour delinquencies are individually acknowledged and handled by the station operators (Ref NFPA 72 section 8.6.3.2.1.5 (6), 2007 edition).

In accordance with UL 827 section 22.1.1. Inspection, testing, RUNNER SERVICE or maintenance shall be permitted to be done by the building or system owner or a person or organization other than the building or system owner if conducted under a written contract in accordance with the National Fire Alarm Code, NFPA 72, 2007 Edition, Section 8.3.2 or the authority having jurisdiction. Please provide documentation of contract for review.

It is required to provide for all aspects of Central Station Service under contract or agreement. This contract shall be in place between the UL Certificate issuing alarm service company (ASC) and the subscriber at the protected property. If any aspect of the service, maintenance or runner response is provided by a third party, a sub-contract for those services shall be in place between the ASC and that third party (NFPA 72, 2007 Edition, Paragraphs 8.3.2., A.8.3.2, and 8.3.3). These contracts shall be made available for review.

It is required to provide for all aspects of Rmote Station Certificates under contract or agreement. This contract shall be in place between the UL Certificate issuing alarm service company (ASC) and the subscriber at the protected property. If any aspect of the service, maintenance or runner response is provided by a third party, a sub-contract for those services shall be in place between the ASC and that third party (NFPA 72, 2007 Edition, Paragraphs 8.3.2., A.8.3.2, and 8.3.3). These contracts shall be made available for review.

Apex Oil Company: Active Supervisory Signals on panel > No AHJ notification >8 hours / Impairment occurred Friday June 3rd, 2022) Upon collaboration with the City of Wilmington Fire Department – Office of the Fire Marshal personnel, a Fire Service Test (FST) was intended at the above protected premise to create a fire alarm signal. Upon arrival and discussion with premise personnel and the review of the installed fire alarm system fire alarm control panel (FACP), an active Supervisory Signal was noted on the panel. Premise personnel provided that the signal was related to a valve supervisory condition where a control valve was recently found to be faulty. Upon further discussion, it was learned that Keller’s personnel worked with the premise owner to bypass the supervisory condition as the replacement control valve was back-ordered and repairs delayed. During this supervisory condition, personnel from Keller’s failed to notify the City of Wilmington Fire Department – Office of the Fire Marshal (designated AHJ) of the described condition upon 8 hours or greater of the impaired fire suppression systems or equipment contrary to Central Station Service requirements. Moreover, please confirm Runner Service was provided in accordance with NFPA 72 via Central Station Service records for the said supervisory signal. The above constitutes a failed FST and non-compliance in accordance with NFPA 72. Please provide correction and verification (NFPA 72-2007: 8.3.7.3 / 8.3.7.3 (2) / 8.3.7.3 (3)).

It appears that the 3 month signal history provided indicates no daily test signals. (Reference: NFPA 72, 2007 Edition, Chapter 8, Paragraph 8.6.3.2.1.5 (6)).

It appears that the 4 month signal history provided indicates daily late to test signals. Please verify that Both phone lines shall be tested at intervals not exceeding 24 hours. (Reference: NFPA 72, 2007 Edition, Chapter 8, Paragraph 8.6.3.2.1.5 (6)).

It appears that the 4 month signal history provided there are multiple phone line fault signals recorded regularly on this account. Please confirm that per NFPA 72, 2007 Edition, section 8.6.3.2.1.1, A DACT shall be connected to the public switched telephone network upstream of any private telephone system at the protected premises. (A) The connections to the public switched telephone network shall be under the control of the subscriber for whom service is being provided by the supervising station fire alarm system. (B) Special attention shall be required to ensure that this connection is made only to a loop start telephone circuit and not to a ground start telephone circuit. As these could be the cause of the telephone signal fault signals.

Please verify that Both phone lines shall be tested at intervals not exceeding 24 hours OR each line is alternately tested every 24 hours. (Reference: NFPA 72, 2007 Edition, Paragraph 8.6.3.2.1.4 B (2)).

If all zones in alarm are not displayed simultaneously, the zone of origin shall be displayed and there shall be an indication that other zones are in alarm. See NFPA 72 sections 4.3.1 and 4.4.4.4 2007 edition.
Detector sensitivity testing shall be performed in accordance with NFPA 72, 2007 Edition, Paragraphs 10.4.4.2 thru 10.4.4.2.6. There was no smoke detector sensitivity testing records on file.

In spaces served by air-handling systems, detectors shall not be located where airflow prevents the operation of the detector (NFPA 72, 2007 Edition, Paragraphs 5.7.4.1 and A.5.7.4.1). The smoke detector in the 5th storage room is located less than 3' from an air supply diffuser.

In spaces served by air-handling systems, detectors shall not be located where airflow prevents the operation of the detector (NFPA 72, 2007 Edition, Paragraphs 5.7.4.1 and A.5.7.4.1). The smoke detector in the control room is located less than 3' from an air supply diffuser.

The duct detector tested(L1D28) did not activate when tested. Please verify proper configuration.

The elevator recall function did not operate when an elevator lobby smoke detector was tested. Please verify proper operation per NFPA 72, 2007 edition, section 6.16.3.12..

Sprinkler valve supervisory switches requires adjustment. It restored to normal with the valve closed and the trip rod seated between the stem threads The off-normal signal shall not be restored at any valve position except normal (NFPA 72, 2007 Edition, Paragraph 5.15.1.3).

Transient voltage primary protector devices shall be installed to circuits that enter or exit a building, and shall be installed in accordance with NFPA 70, Article 800 (NFPA 72, 2007 Edition, Paragraph 4.4.4.3). Please ensure the conductors to the PIV are properly protected.

Upon review of signals from this audit, the Sprinkler Supervisory valve is reporting at the FACP but no supervisory signal was received at the monitoring station as required per NFPA 72 2007 edition section 8.5.2.1.
A Trouble on the NAC panel will not cause a trouble condition at the FACP. All circuits must be Monitored for integrity. Please correct this situation. Ref NFPA 72, 2007 ed, section 4.4.7.1.

A corrective action plan shall be created in order to repair the outstanding trouble condition on the notification appliance power supply located in the west podium storage area. The AHJ should have been notified of the interruption of service. Please provide a copy of the correspondence with the AHJ and an estimated completion date of the repairs. Ref. NFPA 72 (2007) Impairments 10.2.1.2.2 System defects and malfunctions shall be corrected. Ref. NFPA 72 (2007) 8.3.7.4 Trouble Signals. Upon receipt of trouble signals or other signals pertaining solely to matters of equipment maintenance of the fire alarm systems, the central station shall perform the following actions: (1)*Communicate immediately with persons designated by the subscriber (2) Dispatch personnel to arrive within 4 hours to initiate maintenance, if necessary (3) When the interruption is more than 8 hours, provide notice to the subscriber and the fire department if so required by the authority having jurisdiction as to the nature of the interruption, the time of occurrence, and the restoration of service. Ref. NFPA 72 (2007) 4.6* Impairments. 4.6.1 The system owner or their designated representative shall be notified when a fire alarm system or part thereof is impaired. Impairments to systems shall include out-of-service events. 4.6.2 A record shall be maintained by the system owner or designated representative for a period of 1 year from the date the impairment is corrected. 4.6.3* Where required, mitigating measures acceptable to the authority having jurisdiction shall be implemented for the period that the system is impaired. 4.6.4 The system owner or owner’s designated representative shall be notified when an impairment period is completed or discontinued.

In accordance with NFPA 72 2007 edition section 6.8.5.11.1 Automatic fire suppression system alarm-initiating devices and supervisory signal-initiating devices and their circuits shall be designed and installed so that they cannot be subject to tampering, opening, or removal without initiating a signal. NFPA 72, 2007 Edition, section 6.8.5.11.1 Automatic fire suppression system alarm-initiating devices and supervisory signal-initiating devices and their circuits shall be designed and installed so that they cannot be subject to tampering, opening, or removal without initiating a signal. This provision shall include junction boxes installed outside of buildings to facilitate access to the initiating device circuit. Exception No. 1: Covers of junction boxes inside of buildings. There were several signals which exceeded 90 seconds in being received at the station. Please verify the radio is operating correctly per NFPA 72 (2010 edition) section 26.6.3.3.2.2.

We simulated a phone line trouble by disconnecting the primary phone line. After waiting the required time for the panel to indicate a trouble we did not see anything at the keypad. Please provide evidence that the signal was generated by the panel to the monitoring center and change the programming on the control panel to indicate a local trouble for any phone line problem. NFPA 72 2010, 26.6.3.2.1.3* Requirements for DACTs. (D) If the maximum number of attempts to complete the sequence is reached, an indication of the failure shall be made at the premises.

With failure of either of the DACT's communication channels, all change of status signals shall be transmitted to the central station over the second channel, per NFPA72 2010 edition section 26.6.3.2.1.5(1). The secondary phone line was not functioning as signals were not seen at the central station upon the primary phone line removal. Please correct this AND retest to see that they are properly received at the central station.

With failure of either of the DACT's communication channels, all change of status signals shall be transmitted to the central station over the second channel, per NFPA72 2010 edition section 26.6.3.2.1.5(2). The primary phone line was not functioning as signals were not seen at the central station until 4 minutes after the signal was generated. Per section 26.3.1.7, receipt of signal at the station shall not exceed 90 seconds. Please correct this AND retest to see that they are properly received at the central station. Note: If a single communication technology is being used for compliance, then the cellular transmitter must also be configured in compliance with section 26.3.1.4.1.

Best Practices: Please review your operational testing proceedures of smoke detectors in the field is in compliance with the required test methods of NFPA 72 2010 Table 14.4.2.2. (g)Smoke detectors (1) In other than one- and two-family dwellings, system detectors and single- or multiple-station smoke alarms. Smoke detectors/smoke alarms shall be tested in place to ensure smoke entry into the sensing chamber and an alarm response. Testing with smoke or listed aerosol, acceptable to the manufacturer of the aerosol or the manufacturer of the smoke detector/smoke alarm and identified in their published instructions, shall be permitted as acceptable test methods. Other methods listed in the manufacturer’s published instructions that ensure smoke entry from the protected area, through the vents, into the sensing chamber shall be permitted. Any of the following tests shall be performed to ensure that each smoke detector is within its listed and marked sensitivity range: (1) Calibrated test method (2) Manufacturer’s calibrated sensitivity test instrument (3) Listed control equipment arranged for the purpose (4) Smoke detector/control unit arrangement whereby the detector causes a signal at the control unit when its sensitivity is outside its listed sensitivity range (5) Other calibrated sensitivity test method approved by the authority having jurisdiction

NFPA 72 2010 Chapter 14.4.5.3 Please review your operational testing procedures of smoke detectors in the field is in compliance with the required test methods of NFPA 72 2010 Chapter 14.4.5.3.1 - 14.4.5.3.7 Smoke detectors/smoke alarms shall be tested in place to ensure smoke entry into the sensing chamber and an alarm response. 10.5.5.1 Of NFPA 72, 2010 Edition, requires a "dedicated branch circuit" for the fire alarm system. A key requirement is that primary power must be supplied by a dedicated branch circuit. The intent is that no other system or equipment can be powered from the same circuit. So, for example, it would be improper to install an electrical receptacle outlet on the circuit for technicians to use when servicing the system. It is acceptable to remove and/or securely cover the additional outlets in the box near the control so no other device can be plugged into an open outlet.

A separate branch circuit is required for the fire alarm system, remove all AC outlets and equipment connected to the fire alarm system. Refer to NFPA 72, 2010 Edition, Paragraphs 10.5.5.1 through 10.5.5.3.

A 2nd Supplemental Fire Service Test was conducted at this property on Tuesday July 19, 2016 at 9:28 A.M. The result of the service test is as follows: The runner did not arrive within the required response time. Refer to NFPA 72, 2010 Ed. This most recent failed fire service test results in the 3rd consecutive fire service test failure for this service center which indicates the inability to demonstrate that central station service in accordance with the requirements of NFPA 72 is being provided as indicated by a certificate being issued. Due to the failure to consistently demonstrate compliance with the requirements of NFPA 72 we have no choice but to proceed with the withdrawal process of the UL Listing in the UUFX category for this service center. The listing will remain suspended for a period of 30 days during which time the certificate holders will be notified that their certificates are being canceled. At the end of the 30 day period any active certificates will be canceled and the UL Listing withdrawn. This service center may delay the withdrawal of the listing in the UUFX category by submitting an acceptable cause analysis and corrective action plan detailing the cause of the multiple consecutive failures along with the actions taken to avoid the failures moving forward. If an acceptable cause analysis and corrective action plan is received, the next phase will be to demonstrate the ability to again provide central station service in compliance with NFPA 72, on a consistent basis, by successful completion of 2 consecutive fire service tests which would be billed separately as part of a reinstatement project. During this period the service center will remain in the withdrawal process and in a suspended status which means that new certificates cannot be issued and existing certificates cannot be renewed. The procedure that we will follow has been outlined in detail at this time so that there will be no misunderstanding of our intentions. We do hope that you will take immediate steps to improve your compliance with the noted requirements. Should you have any questions please do not hesitate to contact this office.

It is required to provide for all aspects of Central Station Service under contract or agreement. NFPA 72 2010 Edition, Section 26.3.2 and 26.3.3 (3) Service Scope. NFPA 72, 2010 Edition, section 26.3.2 and 26.3.3 Section 26.3 shall apply to central station service, which consists of the following elements: (1) Installation of alarm transmitters (2) Alarm, guard, supervisory, and trouble signal monitoring (3) Retransmission (4) Associated record keeping and reporting (5) Testing and maintenance (6) Runner service The central station service elements shall be provided under contract to a subscriber by one of the following: (3) A listed alarm service–local company that provides the installation, testing, and maintenance with its own facilities and personnel and that subcontracts the monitoring, retransmission, and associated record keeping and reporting to a listed central station. Where located within 2.1 m (7 ft.) of the floor, cables shall be securely fastened in and approved manner at intervals of not more than 450 mm (18 in.). The AES Intelli-Net transmitter antenna cable is supported by being strapped to an existing conduit. Power-limited fire alarm circuit conductors shall not be strapped, taped, or attached by any means to the exterior of any conduit or other raceway as means of support. Reference NFPA 70, 2011 Edition, Section 760.143.

There are fire alarm wirings for the fire alarm Telguard communicator and phone line cable's that are mounted to zip tie mounts that are installed along the finished wall. Cables located within 7' of the floor shall be securely fastened in an approved manner to the building structure at intervals of not more than 18" or otherwise be protected in a raceway or by the building structure, per NFPA 70 (NEC), 2011 Edition, Sections 760.24 and 760.53; NFPA 72, Section 12.2.4 (2013 edition).

Unless prohibited by the Authority Having Jurisdiction, a single transmission path shall be permitted, and the path supervised at an interval of not more than 60 minutes. A failure of the path shall be annunicated at the central station or supervising station within not more than 60 minutes. (NFPA 72, 2013 Edition, Section 10.6.5). 10.6.5.1 Branch Circuit. The branch circuit supplying the fire alarm equipment(s) or emergency communication system(s) shall supply no other loads and shall be supplied by one of the following: (1) Commercial light and power (2) An engine-driven generator or equivalent in accordance with 10.6.11.2, where a person specifically trained in its operation is on duty at all times (3) An engine-driven generator or equivalent arranged for cogeneration with commercial light and power in accordance with 10.6.11.2, where a person specifically trained in its operation is on duty at all times NFPA 72, 2013 Edition, Section 24.5.2.5.5.1 Primary Power Source. The primary power source shall be supplied from a dedicated branch circuit and comply with 10.6.5.1.

The Follow-up Service Agreement that your company executed indicates that the Alarm Service Company assumes full and complete responsibility for its use of the UL Mark, in this case UL Certificates. Managing your customers UL Certificates is necessary to insure that a UL Certificate covers only active systems that comply with AHJ and NFPA 72 requirements and are maintained by your Service Center. In the event a Certificated fire alarm system test and inspection is delinquent or is not able to be brought into compliance with the AHJ and NFPA 72 nation fire alarm and signaling code, The Certificate should be cancelled within 30 days and a new Certificate may be issued when system interruptions are repaired and the fire alarm system has been restored to a normal condition. Maintenance, testing and inspections of the fire alarm system is the responsibility of the property owner or building owner or the owner's designated representative. Ref. NFPA 72 (2013) 14.2.3 Responsibilities. 14.2.3.1* The property or building or system owner or the owner’s designated representative shall be responsible for inspection, testing, and maintenance of the system and for alterations or additions to this system. 14.2.3.2 Where the property owner is not the occupant, the property owner shall be permitted to delegate the authority and responsibility for inspecting, testing, and maintaining the fire protection systems to the occupant, management firm, or managing individual through specific provisions in the lease, written use agreement, or management contract. 14.2.3.3 Inspection, testing, or maintenance shall be permitted to be done by the building or system owner or a person or organization other than the building or system owner if conducted under a written contract. 14.2.3.4 Where the building or system owner has delegated any responsibilities for inspection, testing, or maintenance, a copy of the written delegation required by 14.2.3.3 shall be provided to the authority having jurisdiction upon request.

When the building owner has delegated responsibility to inspect, test and maintain a fire alarm system for their property, it shall be conducted under a written contract. The contract shall be provided to the authority having jurisdiction upon request. Ref. NFPA 72, 2013 Edition, Chapter 14.2.3 Responsibilities

Refer to NFPA 72, 2013 Ed. 26.3.8.1 Because of the failure of the runner to arrive within the required response time a project will be opened to conduct a Supplemental Fire Service Test within the next 60 days. A Supplemental Fire Service Test was condcuted with the AHJ at Learning Care Group, 10900 Nichols Blvd Olive Branch, MS on 12-14-16. A fire alarm signal was initiated at 9:56am and the runner arrived at 10:49am. The runner did not have keys to reset the manual pull and did not have a code to successfully access the reset feature of the system. After obtaining a key from the Fire Marshal who opened the Knox box and obtaining a code from the Assistant Director for the facility, the runner was able to demonstrate the ability to reset the system. The runner shall always have access to the equipment needed to reset an initiating device(s), provided that the device(s) are not damaged and can be reset. Also the runner shall be provided with a satisfactory code for silencing any system needed to be reset. Please keep in mind the following NFPA 72, 2013 Edition Standards for runner service: 3.3.248 Runner Service. The service provided by a runner at the protected premises, including restoration, resetting, and silencing of all equipment transmitting fire alarm or supervisory or trouble signals to an off-premises location. 26.3.8.1.2 The central station shall perform the following actions: (1)*Retransmit the alarm to the communications center in accordance with 26.2.1. (2) Dispatch a runner or technician to the protected premises to arrive within 2 hours after receipt of a signal if equipment needs to be manually reset by the prime contractor. Except where prohibited by the authority having jurisdiction, the runner or technician shall be permitted to be recalled prior to arrival at the premises if a qualified representative of the subscriber at the premises can provide the necessary resetting of the equipment and is able to place the system back in operating condition. (3) Immediately notify the subscriber. (4) Provide notice to the subscriber or authority having jurisdiction, or both, if required. This Fire Service Test is being filed as successful; however future tests could be challenged if the above criteria are not met.

A supplemental Fire Service Test was conducted at this property on Wednesday December 4, 2019 at 9:28 A.M. The result of the service test is as follows: The runner did not arrive within the required response time. Please provide in writing the cause and the resolution. Refer to NFPA 72, 2013 Ed. 26.3.8.1 Because of the failure of the supplemental Fire Service Test, your ability to issue or renew certificates will be suspended and a second project will be opened to conduct another supplemental Fire Service Test within the next 60 days. The cost of the second supplemental Fire Service Test will be $2080.00 plus travel & living expenses. If the second supplemental Fire Service Test also yields a noncompliant result, UL will have no choice but to cancel all your active certificates and start the withdraw process of your company's listing.

During audit review, the provided contract documentation did not indicate system Monitoring, Runner Service or Inspection/Testing/Maintenance (ITM) was provided as required and in accordance with (NFPA 72-2013 Edition: 26.3.2 / 26.3.3). Please provide.

During review of the signal processing history, the system was found to have runaway signals from multiple instances and devices throughout the 90 day history. Runaway signals compromise the integrity of the FACU's ability to send accurate signals, as well as have the ability to compromise the integrity of the monitoring center's processing of signals. Repeated signals are cause for runner service and should be responded to as per NFPA 72 2013, paragraph 23.12.1, and 26.3.8.4. Please assess the cause of these signals and provide with your clearance letter.

NFPA 72, 2013 Edition, section 26.3.2 (6) and 26.3.3 (3) Section 26.3 shall apply to central station service, which consists of the following elements: (6) Runner service The central station service elements shall be provided under contract to a subscriber by one of the following: (3) A listed alarm service–local company that provides the installation, testing, and maintenance with its own facilities and personnel and that subcontracts the monitoring, retransmission, and associated record keeping and reporting to a listed central station with the required runner service provided by the listed alarm service–local company with its own personnel or the listed central station with its own personnel. **During the audit it was found that the executed contracts do not reflect that a runner will be provided. Please either revise the current contract or add an addendum listing runner response as part of the provided central station services.

NFPA 72, 2013 Edition, section 26.3.2 (6) and 26.3.3 It is required to provide for all aspects of Central Station Service under contract or agreement. This contract shall be in place between the UL Certificate issuing alarm service company (ASC) and the subscriber at the protected property. If any aspect of the service, maintenance or runner response is provided by a third party, a sub-contract for those services shall be in place between the ASC and that third party **During the audit it was found that the executed contracts do not reflect that runner service will be provided. Please either revise the current contract or add an addendum listing runner service as part of the provided central station services.

On 08-29-18 an alarm condition was initiated at 8:55am along with the Fire Marshal Ashcraft. The tech did not arrive in the prescribed time to reset the system. The central station received the alarm signal at 8:59am, the fire dispatch center was notified at 8:59am. Although the runner, (Mr. Lowe from Imperial Security) did arrive within the prescribed time 9:26am, he was unaware of the need or the knowledge of how to reset the system. We did await the entire hour for the possibility of an additional runner to arrive. Please provide in writing the cause and resolution. Refer to NFPA 72 2013 Edition, 26.3.8.1.2 (2). Because of the failure of the runner to arrive within the required response time a project will be opened to conduct a Supplemental Fire Service Test within the next 60 days. The cost of the Supplemental Service Test will be $1885.00 plus travel and living expenses that will be invoiced to you in addition to your annual fees paid to maintain your UL Listing. If the Supplemental Fire Service Test also yields noncompliant results, UL can suspend your Listing which includes the ability to issue new or renew existing certificates until such time as effective process changes can demonstrate that compliance has been achieved. *Note: Memphis Fire Dept. has an amended ordinance requiring runner arrival within one (1) hour of an alarm event.
